claim_gate.reason: The exact nonlinear decoder preserves access and avoids a score table, but its interval bounds leave exponentially many candidate evaluations in the observed scaling.

decoder_contract.bound: center score plus the sum of exact per-term Lipschitz variation caps
decoder_contract.correctness: complete search with a rigorous upper bound; no hidden-reflection verification is used
decoder_contract.input: iid random-label quadrature records with computational-basis bad-register rate 1/n
decoder_contract.objective: maximize the exact correlation likelihood over every d in Z_(2^n)
decoder_contract.search: priority branch-and-bound over integer frequency intervals

falsifiers_triggered: Random high-frequency labels saturate the separable Lipschitz bound over broad candidate intervals.
Avoiding an N-entry score table does not help when exact search still evaluates an exponential fraction of candidates.
Finite exact recovery under f=1 contamination is information evidence, not polynomial decoding evidence.
This baseline rejects only the implemented interval bound; stronger nonlinear or algebraic global optimization remains open.
summary: Ran 20 exact branch-and-bound decodes with f=1-rate contamination. Exact reflection recoveries=20; fitted log2 candidate-evaluation slope=1.0000; polynomial branch-and-bound proofs=0.
